summary: Iteration 15 — Brain global/non-repetitive bug fix + Compile Data master tab + CBIC FX (Phase C) fully verified. All 11 pytest cases PASS in 34s and all required frontend flows on /customs-compliance work end-to-end. Brain returns DISTINCT, country-tailored, NON-India-centric answers for Spain→Brazil olive oil, worldwide cocoa, and China→Nigeria electronics. Compile Data returns a real LLM Executive Brief plus duty/tariff-comparison/trade-stats/currency panels for both coffee (IN→DE EUR) and rice (IN→AE AED) lanes. CBIC FX endpoint returns notifiedDate and USD import/export rates. Regression on Trade Stats and Duty & Benefits still green.
critical_code_review_comments: compile_engine.py builds engine_outputs as flattened summary strings only and passes empty `data: {}` to the LLM. Works today, but if downstream consumers expect structured engineOutputs (like /api/brain/ask returns), they'll break. Consider normalizing to the same shape as Brain's orchestrator.

compile_engine.py CACHE keyed on f'{hs6}:{exporter}:{importer}:{currency}' with a 7-day TTL — if duty/RoDTEP/tariff data is refreshed by admin in those 7 days, compile results stay stale. Suggest invalidating compile_cache from /api/duty/refresh too.

customs.py CBIC_NOTIFIED is a hardcoded dict with CBIC_NOTIFIED_DATE='2026-06-19' — Phase C is delivering static notified rates, not a live CBIC notification parser. The endpoint contract says 'notified rates used to assess customs duty' but operations will need a scheduled refresher pulled from CBIC's actual notification PDF; flag this as MOCKED for the Indian customs FX values until a parser is wired in.

brain/router.py extract_entities iterates 4 country source lists (DUTY_COUNTRIES, COUNTRY_PROFILES, COUNTRIES) — duplicate sources can match the same country thrice; the de-dup `not in countries` check prevents pollution but is O(n²). Minor perf only at this scale.
Backend test_iteration_15 (and previous test_phase8/9_brain_live) hit the live LLM on every CI run — consider a fast-mock path or fixture-recording so backend tests don't burn LLM credits.
test_credentials: Not required — all endpoints under test are public. Admin (00001/Shiv@12345) was NOT touched, settings unchanged.
seed_data_creation: None new. Brain queries are cached briefly by Brain layer; compile_cache picks up entries automatically (one entry per lane).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Brain is LIVE (Emergent gpt-5.4-mini) and confirmed non-repetitive + global. If you need to re-verify the bug fix, hit POST /api/brain/ask with three different questions across continents — answers must differ in content AND in `enginesUsed`. Compile Data: GET /api/compile/report?product=<X>&exporter=<iso3num>&importer=<iso3num>&currency=<X>. Cold lane ≈12–25s, cached <1s. The Compile narrative is generated by the LLM via brain.providers.get_provider(); if it ever returns an empty string, check that BRAIN_AI_ENABLED=true and EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is set in backend/.env. CBIC FX values (notifiedDate=2026-06-19) are STATIC in code — flag this as 'partially mocked' if E1 ever claims they're live-fetched.
rca of the issue: Original bug RCA — the Brain previously hardcoded India-centric template strings in providers.py PromptManager.SYSTEM and engines.py (trade_news/market_intel/logistics/policy/tariff) which returned the same paragraphs (e.g. 'India exports crossed $450B', 'Mundra port') regardless of the question's country. Mitigation: PromptManager.SYSTEM was rewritten to enforce country-of-question grounding + anti-repetition, engines were generalized to derive country/product from extract_entities at call time, and router.select_engines was tightened (caps at 5 engines and routes by KEYWORD_ENGINES). Verified: 3 distinct questions return 3 distinct global answers, no India boilerplate leaks into Spain→Brazil or China→Nigeria queries, and `enginesUsed` varies by intent (compliance / trade_statistics / duty_benefits+logistics).
verified_flows: POST /api/brain/ask 'export olive oil Spain to Brazil' → answer mentions Spain/Brazil/olive, no India boilerplate
POST /api/brain/ask 'top importers of cocoa beans worldwide' → trade_statistics engine, cocoa-specific
POST /api/brain/ask 'ship electronics China to Nigeria, duty' → duty_benefits/logistics engines, China/Nigeria mentioned
POST /api/brain/ask 'top exporters of crude oil' → returns Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, UAE etc. — global list, NOT India

GET /api/compile/report?product=coffee&exporter=356&importer=276&currency=EUR → ok:true, hsCode=090122, narrative (Executive Brief) >500 chars, tradeStats+duty+tariffComparison(6 markets)+fx(EUR rate)+price(landedUSD)
GET /api/compile/report?product=rice&exporter=356&importer=784&currency=AED → ok:true with hsCode + UAE importer
GET /api/compile/report (no product, no hs) → ok:false, error message
Frontend /customs-compliance default tab = Compile Data (compile-search visible immediately)

Frontend Compile flow: type 'coffee' → compile-suggestions opens (8) → click suggestion → dropdown CLOSES and stays closed → set exporter India, importer Germany, currency USD → click compile-submit → compile-result renders in <40s with Executive Brief (India→Germany lane, MFN 9% Germany duty, RoDTEP 1.4%, EU food import compliance, German labeling rules, FOB/CIF/DAP routing tips, 3 next steps) + Duty & Benefits panel + Tariff Comparison (UAE 0%, UK 8%, DE 9%, JP 12%, CN 15%) + Trade Statistics (World $801.4M, top importers US/Spain/France/Canada, leading exporters CH/France/Italy/Germany) + Currency & Landed Cost ($12,371.50 landed)
Frontend Trade Statistics regression (customs-tab-trade → coffee → trade-result) — PASS
Frontend Duty & Benefits regression (customs-tab-duty → coffee → duty-submit → duty-result) — PASS
Frontend Compliance Report tab (customs-tab-report) renders without errors
